title: What Will Happen To The Gang Next Year?

jokes.text: Hey! You didn't whisper in my ear and kiss me to wake me up like you always do. / I've never done that.
jokes.setup: Liz expects romantic morning routine from boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Criss has never done this romantic thing she thinks he always does
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Liz's romantic delusions vs reality, economical delivery

jokes.text: You named your van after the guy from Dawson's Creek? / I've seen everything he's ever been in except for that.
jokes.setup: Liz questions van named Van Der Beek
jokes.punchline: Criss claims to have seen everything but the most famous role
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture inversion - knowing everything except the famous thing is unexpected

jokes.text: We don't need a nursery now. And who knows if we'll ever even have a... Plant or whatever?
jokes.setup: Liz avoiding saying 'baby' in serious relationship conversation
jokes.punchline: Substitutes 'plant' as euphemism for baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon avoidance behavior - can't even say 'baby' in baby conversation

jokes.text: Hey! I don't bail. I am still watching Smash, Criss.
jokes.setup: Liz defending herself against accusation of bailing
jokes.punchline: Uses watching terrible TV show as proof of commitment
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly captures Liz's neuroses - staying loyal to bad TV as relationship metaphor

jokes.text: I mean, the people there can be a little sexually conservative for my tastes.
jokes.setup: Hazel explaining why she can't go to homeless shelter
jokes.punchline: Implies homeless shelter residents aren't sexually adventurous enough
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Hazel's twisted priorities and self-absorption

jokes.text: Gonna move to the bay area now, pretend that that was your dream the whole time? Have fun always carrying a light sweater.
jokes.setup: Hazel attacking Jenna's potential life choices
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about San Francisco weather as insult
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational detail that makes geographic stereotype feel fresh and mean

jokes.text: I look forward to reading your obituary, 'Least famous person in the world dies.'
jokes.setup: Hazel delivering parting shot to Jenna
jokes.punchline: Imaginary obituary headline that's cruelly accurate
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted insult that hits Jenna's core insecurity with newspaper headline format

jokes.text: Yes, I married Becky and Dee because love is love and there's no reason they shouldn't experience the joy of marriage like any other couple. / I'm not going to the container store.
jokes.setup: Liz giving heartfelt speech about marriage equality
jokes.punchline: Jack's response completely ignores the sentiment, focuses on mundane errand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - completely missing emotional point for practical concerns

jokes.text: Congratulations. You just turned into your father.
jokes.setup: Liz refusing to help with wedding plans
jokes.punchline: Jack's devastating psychological analysis
jokes.explanation: Jack's manipulation tactic delivered with surgical precision - maximum psychological damage in minimum words

jokes.text: What Bill O'Reilly erotic novel are you living in?
jokes.setup: Liz reacting to Jack's bizarre family situation
jokes.punchline: Combining conservative pundit with erotic fiction genre
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist combination - O'Reilly and erotica is perfect contradiction for 30 Rock sensibility

jokes.text: I suppose our situation is most reminiscent of 'The Commodore's lust' from the 'Patriot's after dark' series.
jokes.setup: Following up on Liz's O'Reilly erotic novel reference
jokes.punchline: Jack knows specific titles from conservative erotica series
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that escalates the absurdity - Jack not only knows the genre but specific titles

jokes.text: And I'm gonna throw that thing in front of a train. Go Phillies!
jokes.setup: Liz negotiating for Yankees bobblehead tickets
jokes.punchline: Plans to destroy Yankees bobblehead, reveals Phillies loyalty
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's petty sports rivalry and willingness to destroy promotional items

jokes.text: Skinny arm-havers!
jokes.setup: Liz insulting Yankees fans
jokes.punchline: Bizarre body-specific insult
jokes.explanation: Weirdly specific insult that feels very Liz Lemon - nonsensical but delivered with conviction

jokes.text: Stop, drop, and roll! Let me out! Let me out!
jokes.setup: Awkward family tension reaching breaking point
jokes.punchline: Diana literally trying to escape using fire safety technique
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat showing family dysfunction through emergency response

jokes.text: Did they say when the banquet is? Because I'm kind of between tuxes due to weight fluctuation.
jokes.setup: Tracy being named man of year by Aryan Patriot Party
jokes.punchline: More concerned about formal wear than racist honor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy obliviousness - completely missing the problem while focusing on clothing logistics

jokes.text: For instance, in Pixar's upcoming movie about trash, I'm doing the voice of a lazy bottle of grape-flavored soda named Funky Bobo.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his positive image representation
jokes.punchline: Example is incredibly stereotypical animated character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Hollywood stereotyping - Tracy thinks he's helping but describes exactly the problem

jokes.text: Okay, I hear it!
jokes.setup: Tracy's oblivious description of stereotypical role
jokes.punchline: Dotcom's immediate realization of the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight man reaction - economical recognition of Tracy's unconscious racism

jokes.text: I was living in an exact replica of Cinderella's castle that Kim Jong-Un had built for his shoe collection.
jokes.setup: Avery describing captivity hardships
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific detail about dictator's shoe storage
jokes.explanation: Surreal detail that combines fairy tale imagery with dictator eccentricity perfectly

jokes.text: And I know a little bit about suffering because I work out... A lot!
jokes.setup: Jenna responding to Avery's traumatic captivity story
jokes.punchline: Compares exercise to actual torture and imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna narcissism - making someone else's trauma about her gym routine

jokes.text: It's a code!
jokes.setup: Avery and Scott's repetitive news broadcast segments
jokes.punchline: Jack's dramatic realization of hidden communication
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment that escalates the captivity subplot with spy thriller elements

jokes.text: Oh, God, I had so many husbands.
jokes.setup: Hazel mentioning having to return to cult upstate
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to polygamous cult marriage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Hazel's disturbing past delivered as casual complaint

jokes.text: Well, where will you sit patiently in the dark while you wait for the next day to start? I mean, sleep?
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about Hazel's sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Describes sleep in existentially depressing terms then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth weirdness - making sleep sound like existential torture then casually correcting

jokes.text: Have a good day, Doris. / [High-pitched] 'You have a good day too, Kenneth.' / Whatever, Doris. Grow up.
jokes.setup: Kenneth apparently talking to roommate Doris
jokes.punchline: He's voicing both sides of conversation, then dismissing his own creation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth insanity - creating imaginary roommate then getting annoyed with her politeness

jokes.text: I don't mean it, I nice it.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering to let Hazel stay with him
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'nice it' as opposite of 'mean it'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth linguistic innocence - creating logical but nonsensical word construction

jokes.text: You love wet feet?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to decode Avery's messages from Scott
jokes.punchline: Completely misinterprets romantic subtext as foot fetish
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jack's literal interpretation of coded messages

jokes.text: Check out Kim Jong-Un's pants. Where's the flood?
jokes.setup: Scott's coded message to Avery
jokes.punchline: Making fun of dictator's high-water pants in secret communication
jokes.explanation: Clever detail showing captives maintaining humor through fashion mockery of captor

jokes.text: ♪ My soil's pretty good so I will plant this seed ♪ ... ♪ Growing together ♪ ♪ Planty and me ♪
jokes.setup: Liz singing gardening song about her relationship
jokes.punchline: Earnest folk song with terrible lyrics about plant/baby metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz awkwardness - turning relationship anxiety into terrible folk song

jokes.text: [Shouting in Hebrew]
jokes.setup: Criss selling hot dogs at Jewish memorial parade
jokes.punchline: Getting yelled at in Hebrew for inappropriate hot dog sales
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Criss's cultural insensitivity at memorial event

jokes.text: I had no idea the shmuley Israel memorial parade was a predominantly Jewish event.
jokes.setup: Criss explaining why hot dog sales went poorly
jokes.punchline: Claiming ignorance about obviously Jewish memorial being Jewish
jokes.explanation: Good Criss obliviousness - missing extremely obvious cultural context

jokes.text: You know what kind of women in their 40s have never been married, Liz? Uggos, crazies, and bailers. You're not an uggo.
jokes.setup: Criss categorizing unmarried women in their 40s
jokes.punchline: Eliminates 'ugly' but leaves 'crazy' and 'bailer' on the table
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured insult disguised as compliment - eliminates least offensive category

jokes.text: [Gasps, snorts, groans] / Doris! / Wonderful. See you tonight, roomie.
jokes.setup: Kenneth doing impression while Hazel watches
jokes.punchline: Hazel correctly identifies it as 'Doris' character
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Kenneth's imaginary roommate with Hazel immediately understanding the game

jokes.text: I'd admire you if you weren't brunette.
jokes.setup: Jenna analyzing Hazel's manipulative behavior
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary hair color discrimination undermines potential respect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna shallowness - hair color as moral determining factor

jokes.text: I'm a natural blonde. / That's insane!
jokes.setup: Hazel revealing she's naturally blonde
jokes.punchline: Jenna's shocked reaction to hair color revelation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jenna's hair-based prejudices with appropriate shock

jokes.text: Stop looking at my ass. / Stop pointing your ass at my eyeballs.
jokes.setup: Hazel accusing Jenna of staring
jokes.punchline: Jenna's defensive counter-accusation about ass positioning
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure with visual comedy element

jokes.text: It's an honor to finally meet you, Questlove. / Tracy, this is Dr. Cornel West.
jokes.setup: Tracy meeting important African American intellectual
jokes.punchline: Mistakes prestigious academic for drummer from The Roots
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy obliviousness showing his limited cultural knowledge despite meeting role models

jokes.text: Look, I don't wanna make us look bad, but these dumb white writers don't know how us soul cats speak, one 'twixt the other.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his image problem
jokes.punchline: Blames writers while using fake period slang, breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that breaks fourth wall while having Tracy use ridiculous fake slang

jokes.text: Who were your black role models growing up? / Darth Vader, ninjas, some black licorice I tried to make into the shape of my dad.
jokes.setup: Serious question about positive role models
jokes.punchline: Tracy lists increasingly absurd non-human black things
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from pop culture villains to literal candy crafting - perfect Tracy logic

jokes.text: Under my own nose. Like a mustache. A mustache. Tom Selleck!
jokes.setup: Tracy interpreting metaphor about role models being close
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation leads to Tom Selleck as black role model
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy literal thinking leading to completely wrong but logical conclusion

jokes.text: One day we made love while tapping. It was beautiful. No butt stuff.
jokes.setup: Scott describing romantic connection with Avery
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specific denial about sexual practices
jokes.explanation: Good awkward oversharing that makes romantic moment uncomfortable

jokes.text: Jack and Diana... Avery! Avery and Diana... Damn it! Jack and Nancy.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to remember character names in crisis
jokes.punchline: Panic-induced confusion about who's married to whom
jokes.explanation: Good Liz panic moment showing stress through character name confusion

jokes.text: Kim Jong-Il is dead. I'm only waiter. I'm greatest waiter of all time.
jokes.setup: Waiter being questioned about wedding disruption
jokes.punchline: Claims innocence then immediately brags about superior waitering
jokes.explanation: Good random character moment - denying involvement then boasting about unrelated skill

jokes.text: He said that to me in Galveston when a busload of drunk debutantes tried to get on his boat. It was named 'Mr. water boat.' Did it sink? Yes. Because there were too many people on it.
jokes.setup: Jack quoting Bush's 'bring 'em on' in context
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific backstory ending with boat sinking
jokes.explanation: Great Jack storytelling with perfect absurd details and anticlimatic ending

jokes.text: Oh, are you talking about Hazel or the raccoon that won't let me into my kitchen?
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about Kenneth's roommate situation
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions aggressive raccoon as equally problematic roommate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdity - treating wild animal invasion as normal roommate issue

jokes.text: And to think I thought Hazel was a bitch! / Friendly and loyal like a well-trained female dog. But she isn't a bitch. She's a meaniepants.
jokes.setup: Kenneth learning Hazel sabotaged him
jokes.punchline: Literal dog interpretation of 'bitch' then childish alternative insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth innocence - taking curse word literally then using kindergarten insult

jokes.text: A guy with two first names? Next.
jokes.setup: Dotcom suggesting Frederick Douglass as role model
jokes.punchline: Tracy dismisses civil rights leader over naming convention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy superficiality - rejecting historical figure for arbitrary reason

jokes.text: I'm not talking about her. I'm talking about me looking funny in this reflection. I'm gonna do a movie where I play someone's fat old aunt and I say stuff like, 'whoo-wee!' And 'sweet child, sit yo' black ass down!'
jokes.setup: Tracy finding his role model inspiration in museum
jokes.punchline: Chooses to emulate Tyler Perry's problematic stereotype movies
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Hollywood stereotyping with Tracy completely missing the point of role models

jokes.text: Why didn't you take out the cameras and kill all the witnesses, you beautiful bastard?
jokes.setup: Liz thinks Criss is a bank robber
jokes.punchline: She critiques his criminal methodology while calling him beautiful
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation showing Liz getting into the criminal fantasy while maintaining romantic tone.

jokes.text: You can't go to prison, you idiot. They will ravage you and your beautiful woman face.
jokes.setup: Liz thinking Criss robbed a bank
jokes.punchline: Warns about prison rape while complimenting his feminine features
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing genuine concern with backhanded compliment about attractiveness

jokes.text: I didn't Rob a bank, Liz. God! No, I sold the van this morning... To a really nice young guy on Meth.
jokes.setup: Criss explaining bank robbery misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Reveals he sold van to meth user, describing him as 'nice'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Criss's naive assessment of drug user as 'nice young guy'

jokes.text: I don't wanna wait for our lives to be over.
jokes.setup: Criss explaining decision to sell van
jokes.punchline: Quotes Dawson's Creek theme song in context of van named after cast member
jokes.explanation: Brilliant multi-layered callback combining van name, show reference, and romantic context perfectly

jokes.text: Have you ever had a girlfriend willing to Thelma and Louise with you? / No. Never. But come on, Bonnie and Clyde. Okay, I'm a boy. I'm a boy, Liz.
jokes.setup: Liz referencing female outlaw movie duo
jokes.punchline: Criss correcting to mixed-gender criminals, emphasizing his masculinity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Criss asserting masculinity while accepting criminal partnership

jokes.text: Also, I hear Pippa Middleton couldn't come today because Avery was borrowing her ass.
jokes.setup: Jenna making pre-ceremony small talk
jokes.punchline: Insults Avery by suggesting she borrowed famous posterior
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna cattiness using topical celebrity reference for body-shaming joke

jokes.text: I just want alereeb to be happy.
jokes.setup: Mystery person speaking at wedding
jokes.punchline: Waiter speaking in broken English about wanting couple happy
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment with linguistic confusion adding to wedding chaos

jokes.text: Are you people kidding me? Seriously, no one has anything to say? You're all cowards.
jokes.setup: Liz frustrated that no one is objecting
jokes.punchline: Calling wedding guests cowards for not disrupting marriage
jokes.explanation: Good irony with officiant encouraging objections to her own ceremony

jokes.text: I played Avery Jessup in 'Kidnapped by danger,' now available on Sega Genesis.
jokes.setup: Jenna finally speaking up at wedding
jokes.punchline: Promotes her TV movie during marriage ceremony with outdated gaming reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - using wedding objection time for self-promotion with anachronistic tech reference

jokes.text: I kept my sea monkeys alive for 30 years.
jokes.setup: Avery explaining her inability to quit things
jokes.punchline: Uses childhood pet maintenance as evidence of perseverance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail showing Avery's competitive nature applied to novelty pets

jokes.text: I mean, if we wanted this thing to succeed, why would we ask Liz to officiate?
jokes.setup: Avery analyzing their subconscious sabotage
jokes.punchline: Insulting Liz's competence as evidence of self-sabotage
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-joke that insults Liz while revealing character psychology

jokes.text: [Laughing] Yeah! Divorce! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Liz completing divorce ceremony
jokes.punchline: Celebrating divorce with inappropriate enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz's inappropriate but honest reaction to chaos resolution

jokes.text: It started that way. But then I fell in love with you.
jokes.setup: Kenneth confronting Hazel about sabotage
jokes.punchline: Hazel's confession pivots to romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Good twist revealing Hazel's motivation change from manipulation to genuine feeling

jokes.text: Don't you ever do that again. / [Both moaning]
jokes.setup: Kenneth rejecting Hazel's advance
jokes.punchline: Immediate sexual response contradicting his words
jokes.explanation: Standard contradiction between words and actions in sexual tension scene

jokes.text: You said, 'baby,' instead of 'plant.' Now say, 'man cave.' / Never!
jokes.setup: Criss noting Liz's progress in saying 'baby'
jokes.punchline: Liz draws line at masculine domestic terminology
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Liz's selective progress in relationship terminology

jokes.text: Because we are thinking about having a baby... Together... That will emerge from my vagina. / Or a Chinese vagina.
jokes.setup: Liz awkwardly explaining baby plans to Jack
jokes.punchline: Criss adding adoption option in equally awkward terms
jokes.explanation: Good awkwardness escalation with couple's clumsy announcement including adoption consideration

jokes.text: But any child would be lucky to have you be the mother it loves then hates for a few years, then loves again, then half-heartedly defends to its spouse, then puts in a home.
jokes.setup: Jack giving Liz encouragement about motherhood
jokes.punchline: Describes entire cynical lifecycle of parent-child relationship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack cynicism - encouraging speech that traces complete relationship decay with perfect escalation

jokes.text: Number one, don't overthink the names. Stick to Kings and Queens of England. There will never be a president Ashton or a Dr. Katniss. Or a non-sexually confused Lorne.
jokes.setup: Jack beginning his 25 pillars of motherhood
jokes.punchline: Specific examples including dig at Lorne Michaels
jokes.explanation: Great Jack advice with pop culture examples and subtle NBC boss reference

jokes.text: All I want is Jack and Liz get together. On Friends, it was so satisfying. They do on Cheers, they do on Moonlighting. Everybody do it! Don't overthink it, writers. Whoever you are.
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing shipping desires for main characters
jokes.punchline: Direct address to writers with sitcom relationship examples and confused identity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary breaking fourth wall with Tracy as fan giving writing advice
